verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Policy declared in config; Guard enforces it; Reviewable in repo; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gate executed red with the policy artifact and consumption absent (3/3 failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.policy_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/update_governance_policy.json declares the full governance surface: five hold classes (linux-image/headers/modules, snapd, accountsservice) each bound to active-session scope with the ARCHBP-111 root apply mechanism named and its 109-map incident evidence cited; the gate mechanism (os-update-hold lease via the T8 plane + the generated Pre-Invoke hook); and the reboot-intent policy (auto_reboot forbidden, ARCHBP-112 explicit-intent applier, post-reboot re-attach units).
gate_result.enforcement_evidence: scripts/os-update-observer.mjs gate-check now CONSUMES the declared policy (default: the committed file; --policy= for review): proven live with the SAME os-update-hold lease held, a policy with gate.enabled=true blocks (exit 1) and a policy with gate.enabled=false allows (exit 0) — behavior flips purely by config, no code edit, and the verdict cites policy_source and policy_gate_enabled.
gate_result.review_evidence: The policy is a committed, schema-versioned artifact; the gate cross-checks that every held class traces to a desktop-breaking pattern in the 109 lifecycle map (evidence-cited, not invented) and that the guard's default policy source is the committed file itself.
gate_result.honest_boundary: Root materialization of the declared holds (apt-mark) and reboot policy (Unattended-Upgrade::Automatic-Reboot) remains ARCHBP-111/112 — the declaration and its unprivileged enforcement point are complete; the root appliers consume the same file.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The enforcement proof executed against the real T8 lease machinery with real hold/release cycles and the real committed policy file.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-116 is complete: update governance is encoded as a declared, reviewable, schema-versioned config that the ARCHBP-110 guard consumes — the same live hold blocks or passes purely by the declared policy, every held class cites its incident evidence, and the root appliers (ARCHBP-111/112) materialize the same file rather than manual steps.
